do_not_merge.reason: Different entity_type and identity: Polly Trottenberg is a person/commissioner affiliated with NYC DOT, while entity_nyc-dot is the New York City Department of Transportation agency. The shared NYC DOT key is affiliation context, not person-agency identity.

Different people/contact mentions in tsp_status_2017. Polly Trottenberg is identified as commissioner, while Scott Gastel and Alana Morales appear as press contacts. A shared NYC DOT affiliation key is not personal identity.

Different entity_type and identity: entity_nyc-dot is the NYC DOT agency, while Scott Gastel and Alana Morales are press contacts affiliated with the agency. The shared NYC DOT key is affiliation context, not identity.

suggested_rules: Do not merge person/contact records with agency records based solely on a shared agency-name key; agency names on people are affiliation context, not identity aliases.
Two person/contact records sharing only an agency affiliation key are not the same identity.
Keep affiliation/context keys out of identity aliases unless the alias is reviewed as an actual name for the same record.
rationale: The do-not-merge decisions from the model suggestion are data-backed and should be promoted. The weak aliases are intentionally removed because promoting the agency key onto person/contact records would preserve the same identity collision. The Scott Gastel/Alana Morales record remains a modeling smell because the source has two press contacts on one line, but that does not block this identity suppression.
